revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:ボケ山古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|image=File:Bokeyama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|location={{ill|Fujiidera|qid=Q634011|1=Fujiidera}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.55643056|N|135.5965|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|architectural_styles={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|qid=Q11268718|1=Zenpōkōenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}}| Interred_individual = {{ill|Emperor Ninken|qid=Q357266|1=Emperor Ninken}}|part_of={{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|qid=Q4250076|1=Furuichi Kofun Cluster}}}}
[[File:851015 Bokeyama Kofun aerial.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Aerial view of Bokeyama Kofun|qq=}}{{Small|{{National Land Image Information|qq=}}.|qq=}}]] [[File:Bokeyama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Kofun (front mound on the right, rear mound in the back left)|qq=}}]] '''Bokeyama Kofun''' (ボケ山古墳, also known as '''Nonaka Bokeyama Kofun''') is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|qid=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} located in {{ill|Fujiidera|en|Fujiidera|qid=Q634011|lt=Fujidera, Osaka|1=Fujiidera}}, Osaka Prefecture, Aoyama 3-chome. It is part of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|en|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|qid=Q4250076|lt=Furuichi Kofun Cluster|1=Furuichi Kofun Cluster}}. The actual burial individual is unknown, but the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|qid=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency|1=Imperial Household Agency}} designates it as "'''Hanyū Sakamoto Mausoleum'''" (埴生坂本陵), attributed to {{ill|Emperor Ninken|en|Emperor Ninken|qid=Q357266|lt=Emperor Ninken|1=Emperor Ninken}}. == Overview == Bokeyama Kofun measures 122 meters in total length, with the front mound 107 meters wide and 13 meters high, and the rear mound 65 meters in diameter and dd.5 meters high. It features a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|qid=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} design, with a surrounding moat. The southeastern constricted area includes a {{ill|Tsukuridashi|lt=protrusion|ja|造出|1=Platform-style projection|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukuridashi|13=User:Immanuelle/Platform-style projection|qq=}}, and cylindrical haniwa rows were discovered on the embankments during investigations by the Osaka Prefectural Board of Education in 1975 and 1979. Additionally, in 1981, the Habikino City Board of Education uncovered two haniwa kilns on the slope adjacent to the northwest embankment of the front mound. Based on the characteristics of the excavated cylindrical haniwa, the tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the early 6th century. The site is managed by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|qid=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency|1=Imperial Household Agency}}, which designates it as the mausoleum of Emperor Ninken. A {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=worship site|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} is located outside the moat at the front mound's central area. <gallery> File:Bokeyama Kofun, haisho.jpg|{{ill|Emperor Ninken|en|Emperor Ninken|qid=Q357266|lt=Emperor Ninken|1=Emperor Ninken}} Hanyū Sakamoto Mausoleum {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=Worship Site|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} </gallery> == External Links == {{Commonscat|Bokeyama Kofun|qq=}} * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/024/index.html Hanyū Sakamoto Mausoleum] - Imperial Household Agency {{Coord|34|33|23.15|N|135|35|47.40|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=title|name=Bokeyama Kofun (Attributed to Emperor Ninken)|qq=}}
{{Kofun navbar|qq=}}
